cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the options given. The task is to determine which statement is wrong according to the news provided. The news article states: "America's space program will go on," said a grim-faced Bush in a message broadcast on television. So, the U.S. government, represented by President Bush, is stating that the space program will continue despite the accident. Therefore, option (A) which says "will give up the space program because of the accident" is incorrect according to the news. The article directly states the opposite. The news also states: "NASA launched an investigation into the disaster and began searching for the astronauts' remains. It said that although there had been some data failures it was too early to nail down a precise cause." This means that NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster, making option (B) correct, not the wrong one as the question asks. The news says: "Take-off and re-entry into Earth's atmosphere are the most dangerous parts of a space mission. In 42 years of US' human space flight, there had never been an accident in the descent to Earth or landing. Challenger exploded just after take-off."
